OPPOSING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER BLUE'S ROOFING COMPANY'S MOTION IN SUPPORT OF PETITIONER'S SECOND AMENDED VERIFIED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED REQUESTING REVERSAL OF THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DECISION OF JULY 22, 2004. ALTHOUGH PETITIONER DOES NOT ADDRESS THE STANDARD OF REVIEW, PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN WHETHER THE STANDARD BE ARBITRARY OR CAPRICIOUS OR AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION OR SUBSTANTIAL DEFERENCE. PETITIONER DOES NOT DISPUTE THE APPLICABILITY OF RULE 3(F) BUT IT SEEKS EQUITABLE RELIEF BASED ON STAR ROOFING AND/OR THE USE OF PETITIONER'S DATA. STAR ROOFING IS LIMITED TO PRE DECEMBER 31, 2003 EXPERIENCE MODIFICATION DATA. RULE 3(F), APPLICABLE TO POST JANUARY 1, 2004 DATA DETERMINATIONS, WAS ADOPTED AFTER PUBLIC HEARINGS. IT REFLECTS A BALANCE BETWEEN THE NEED FOR DATA FOR AN EQUITABLE STATEWIDE EXPERIENCE RATING SYSTEM AND THE INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYER'S NEED FOR AN EXPERIENCE RATING THAT REFLECTS ITS OWN EXPERIENCE. THE RULE IS PARTICULARLY APPLICABLE WHERE, AS HERE, THE INSOLVENT CARRIER HAS PROVIDED LITTLE OR NO DATA. ALTHOUGH THE WCIRB IS AUTHORIZED TO "VERIFY ANY AND ALL DATA", IT IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO IGNORE RULE 3(F) TO EXCLUDE DATA OF AN INSOLVENT CARRIER. REGARDING THE USE OF PETITIONER'S DATA, THE VERY CASE UPON WHICH IT RELIES, STAR ROOFING, REJECTS SUCH DATA AS "NOT SUFFICIENTLY RELIABLE." MR. LEW IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DAVID LEW, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (STATE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OFFICE, 510.622.2225) APPEARING FOR RESPONDENT THE INSURANCE COMMISSIONER. THOMAS E. McDONALD, ESQUIRE (SONNENSCHEIN, 415.882.1088) APPEARING FOR REAL-PARTY-IN-INTEREST W.C.I.R.B.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING:
THE WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED REQUESTING REVERSAL OF THE DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DECISION OF JULY 22, 2004. ALTHOUGH PETITIONER DOES NOT ADDRESS THE STANDARD OF REVIEW, PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN WHETHER THE STANDARD BE ARBITRARY OR CAPRICIOUS OR AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION OR SUBSTANTIAL DEFERENCE.
PETITIONER DOES NOT DISPUTE THE APPLICABILITY OF RULE 3(F) BUT IT SEEKS EQUITABLE RELIEF BASED ON STAR ROOFING AND/OR THE USE OF PETITIONER'S DATA.
STAR ROOFING IS LIMITED TO PRE DECEMBER 31, 2003 EXPERIENCE MODIFICATION DATA. RULE 3(F), APPLICABLE TO POST JANUARY 1, 2004 DATA DETERMINATIONS, WAS ADOPTED AFTER PUBLIC HEARINGS. IT REFLECTS A BALANCE BETWEEN THE NEED FOR DATA FOR AN EQUITABLE STATEWIDE EXPERIENCE RATING SYSTEM AND THE INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYER'S NEED FOR AN EXPERIENCE RATING THAT REFLECTS ITS OWN EXPERIENCE. THE RULE IS PARTICULARLY APPLICABLE WHERE, AS HERE, THE INSOLVENT CARRIER HAS PROVIDED LITTLE OR NO DATA. ALTHOUGH THE WCIRB IS AUTHORIZED TO "VERIFY ANY AND ALL DATA", IT IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO IGNORE RULE 3(F) TO EXCLUDE DATA OF AN INSOLVENT CARRIER.
REGARDING THE USE OF PETITIONER'S DATA, THE VERY CASE UPON WHICH IT RELIES, STAR ROOFING, REJECTS SUCH DATA AS "NOT SUFFICIENTLY RELIABLE." MR. LEW IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AP) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
